channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Hermes AI agent used to automate attack on Thai Finance Ministry
item.description: A threat actor used the open-source Hermes AI agent in unattended "YOLO" mode to automate post-exploitation activity during an alleged breach of Thailand's Ministry of Finance. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers hijack hotel Wi-Fi DNS to steal Microsoft 365 accounts
item.description: Hackers are changing the DNS settings on Wi-Fi devices at hotels and conference centers to redirect users to fake Microsoft 365 login pages. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft blames massive Microsoft 365 outage on maintenance bug
item.description: Microsoft says a bug in its automated network maintenance request system caused Thursday's massive outage by mistakenly removing IP routes from more devices than intended, disrupting Azure and Microso...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: BlueNoroff Zoom Phishing Kit Profiles Crypto Wallets Before Malware Delivery
item.description: The North Korean threat actors behind the ClickFix-style campaigns that employ typosquatted Zoom and Microsoft Teams domains have been found to operate an active phishing kit to impersonate the videoc...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Certighost Exploit Lets Low-Privileged Active Directory Users Impersonate a Domain Controller
item.description: Researchers H0j3n and Aniq Fakhrul published a working exploit on July 24 that lets a low-privileged Active Directory user obtain a certificate for a Domain Controller and authenticate as that machine...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Chick-fil-A data breach affects more than 13,000 customers
item.description: Chick-fil-A has confirmed that over 13,000 customers had their accounts breached in a wave of credential stuffing attacks targeting its website and mobile app between June 17 and June 19. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Slopsquatting, Phantom Domains, and HalluSquatting Are the Same AI Attack
item.description: Slopsquatting, phantom squatting, and HalluSquatting all exploit the same late-binding attack pattern, where AI coding agents trust hallucinated package, repo, or domain names. ActiveState explains ho...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Vatican's Official Prayer App Leaks 700K+ Global Users' PII
item.description: A porous API endpoint exposes, names, email addresses, country, and site status, all of which can be easily gleaned by anyone with a browser....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Europol flags 4,340 URLs for removal in 'The Com' crackdown
item.description: Europol has flagged 4,340 URLs for removal during a multi-week operation targeting online content linked to "The Com," a loosely organized network of nihilistic violent extremist groups. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Default Azure Automation Setting Enables Cross-Tenant Identity Takeover
item.description: Microsoft addresses a public-by-default configuration and chain of code flaws in Azure Automation that could have let attackers seize another tenant's identity and access others' data, credentials, an...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ChatGPT AgentForger Flaw Could Deploy Rogue Workspace Agents via a Phishing Link
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical vulnerability in OpenAI's ChatGPT Workspace Agents that could have allowed a single phishing link to stealthily build, authorize, and deploy an auto...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Bing Images Flaws Let Crafted SVGs Run Commands as SYSTEM on Microsoft's Servers
item.description:
A crafted SVG submitted to Bing's image search ran commands as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM on Microsoft's production image-processing workers, and as root on the Linux machines in the same fleet.
XBOW's test...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Seeing AI Agents Is Not Enough. Security Teams Must Enforce What They Can Do
item.description: AI agent security is moving through a familiar maturity curve: adoption, then visibility, and finally, control. But what we've collectively discovered is that enforcing least privilege for AI agents i...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Man gets six years for hacking 750 women's Snapchat accounts
item.description: An Illinois man was sentenced on Tuesday to 76 months in prison and three years of supervised release for hacking the Snapchat accounts of over 750 women to steal nude photos. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hacker Runs Hermes AI Agent Unattended for Post-Exploitation at Thai Finance Ministry
item.description: Someone installed a popular AI assistant on a rented server, switched off the setting that makes it ask permission before running risky commands, and pointed it at Thailand's Ministry of Finance, whic...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Golden Chickens Resurfaces With Four New Malware Families and Modular Implants
item.description: The threat actors behind the Golden Chickens malware-as-a-service (MaaS) ecosystem have resurfaced with four new malware families, indicating that the operators are showing no signs of stopping despit...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: NodeBB Patches Eight AI-Found Flaws Exposing Admin Access and Private Chats
item.description: Eight security flaws in NodeBB went public on Wednesday, along with the code to exploit them. Aikido Security rates all eight as high severity and says its AI pentest agents found them in a six-hour r...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Clop ransomware targets Windchill, FlexPLM in data theft attacks
item.description: The Clop ransomware gang (also tracked as Cl0p) is targeting Internet-exposed PTC Windchill and FlexPLM instances in a new data theft extortion campaign. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Europe's Multilingual Reality Exposes AI Security Gaps
item.description: The AI security layer and guardrails for many AI products don't evenly protect against jailbreaking and unsafe actions in every single language....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Kimi K3 Agents Found Redis Zero-Days and Built RCE Exploit, Researchers Say
item.description:
Redis shipped seven security releases on July 23 after researchers published authenticated RCE PoCs for stock Redis 6.2.22, 7.4.9, 8.6.4, and 8.8.0.
All four chains require RESTORE. The Streams chain...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Fake Notepad++ Plugin Delivers MATCHBOIL.V2 in UAC-0099 Attacks
item.description: The Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) has warned of a new campaign that involves the use of a malicious program that's dressed up as a Notepad++ plugin to compromise Windows system...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Russian Hackers Exploit Zimbra Zero-Day Against US, Ukraine Targets
item.description: A state-sponsored threat group, dubbed "Laundry Bear," sends "half-click" phishing emails that require a victim only to open or preview the message....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Dolphin X malware uses AI to rank high-value targets
item.description: A new Dolphin X remote access trojan claims to use an AI-powered profiling feature to score and rank infected users, helping cybercriminals identify which victims should be targeted first. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Australian energy provider Origin says data breach exposes client data
item.description: Origin Energy has confirmed that an unauthorized party accessed and subsequently leaked customer data online, exposing sensitive personally identifiable information (PII), among others. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake Claude app promoted by Bing ads pushes SectopRAT malware
item.description: A malvertising campaign on the Bing search service is pushing a fake Claude desktop app installer hosted on a legitimate Claude.ai domain to deliver the SectopRAT malware. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Russian Espionage Group Exploited Zimbra Zero-Day to Steal Mail and 2FA Codes
item.description:
A Russian state-supported espionage group spent months reading Western mailboxes through a then-unknown flaw in Zimbra's webmail client.
The payload goes after the last 90 days of email, the organiza...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Russian hackers exploit Zimbra zero-click flaw for email theft
item.description: CISA is warning that the Russian state-sponsored hacking group Laundry Bear, also known as Void Blizzard, is targeting organizations using Zimbra Collaboration email servers by combining phishing atta...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers abuse Notepad++ plugins to stealthily install malware
item.description: Ukraine's CERT has uncovered attacks distributing an archive containing the legitimate Notepad++ application and a malicious utility called LunchPoke disguised as a plugin to establish persistence. [....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft 365 outage affects Teams, SharePoint and other services
item.description: Microsoft is impacted by a massive outage affecting Teams and Microsoft 365 services, primarily affecting users in North America. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ThreatsDay: Android Spyware, PLC Attacks, AI Image Prompt Injection + 12 More Stories
item.description:
Most of this week's trouble came dressed as something useful.
A package stole data. A fake extension opened remote access. A safety app became spyware. An image gave hidden orders to an AI agent. Oth...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
